title: principles
description: Coding principles and best practices for writing clean, efficient, and maintainable code across different programming languages.

content.text: This page outlines key coding principles and best practices that are essential for writing clean, efficient, and maintainable code across different programming languages.
A class or module should have only one reason to change, meaning it should have only one responsibility or job.
Software entities (classes, modules, functions) should be open for extension but closed for modification.
Objects of a superclass should be replaceable with objects of a subclass without affecting the correctness of the program.
Clients should not be forced to depend on interfaces they do not use. Instead, many specific interfaces are better than one general interface.
High-level modules should not depend on low-level modules. Both should depend on abstractions. Abstractions should not depend on details. Details should depend on abstractions.
